papers.title: Accounting for Cryptocurrencies and Blockchain Technology: A Literature Review
papers.abstract: Blockchain technology is identified as a disruptive force in the technological landscape, impacting the finance and accounting sectors, wherein cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum challenge traditional strategies of handling and tracking economic transactions. This study examines how blockchain and cryptocurrencies are reshaping accounting practices and explores how conventional accounting frameworks can adapt to these virtual improvements. The study also explores the implications of blockchain for auditing, offering capability advantages including more desirable data integrity and non-stop auditing capabilities.
papers.file: /research/Accounting for Cryptocurrencies   and   Blockchain Technology  A Literature Review         Spring 2024.pdf

papers.title: The Distribution Pattern of Calcification of the Coronary Arteries Among Diabetic and Non-Diabetic Patients
papers.abstract: This study explores the calcification dispersion pattern of the coronary arteries among diabetic and non-diabetic patients. Around 200 cases were gathered from Careful Specialty Cardiovascular Center, Erbil, Kurdistan Region-Iraq. The investigation found that just 88 out of 200 cases had diabetes. The predominance of calcified coronary corridors was seen as higher among non-diabetic compared with diabetic patients. Risk factors such as hypertension, obesity, smoking, hypercholesterolemia, and family ancestry were found to contribute to cardiovascular disease.
papers.file: /research/  THE DISTRIBUTION PATTERN OF CALCIFICATION    OF    THE CORONARY ARTERIES AMONG DIABETIC AND NON-DIABETIC PATIENTS.   .pdf

papers.title: The Impact of High Performance Works Practice on Employees' Job, Life and Career Satisfaction
papers.abstract: This study explores the association between high-performance work practices (HPWPs) — encompassing training, empowerment, rewards, and teamwork — and the levels of personal career, job, and life satisfaction among full-time academic staff at Komar University of Science and Technology. Employing a quantitative methodology with a survey questionnaire and a sample of 92 participants, data was analysed using SPSS v26. The analysis concluded a lack of significant correlation between HPWPs and satisfaction variables, with only 7% of employees in high-performance work reporting satisfaction, challenging initial hypotheses.
papers.file: /research/THE IMPACT OF HIGH PERFORMANCE WORKS PRACTICE ON EMPLOYEES JOB, LIFE AND CAREER SATISFACTION .pdf

papers.title: Hypercalcemia Mediated by Vitamin D: Etiology, Diagnosis, and Therapeutic Approaches
papers.abstract: Hypercalcemia, characterized by an elevated calcium concentration in the blood, is one of the most prominent conditions in the medical profession. This paper acknowledges the risks of hypercalcemia being mediated by vitamin D while also exploring the therapeutic applications of vitamin D in managing the condition. The approach considers the interplay of vitamin D and calcium metabolism, ensuring that the potential benefits of vitamin D are harnessed while addressing the risks of hypercalcemia through nuanced therapy and treatment plans.
papers.file: /research/Hypercalcemia Mediated by Vitamin D Etiology, Diagnosis, and Therapeutic Approaches.pdf

papers.title: Consequences of Servant Leadership on Service Recovery Performance and Bullying
papers.abstract: This study investigates the effect of servant leadership on service recovery performance and workplace bullying among employees in car service centers in Sulaymaniyah City, Kurdistan Region of Iraq. Using a quantitative survey design with a sample of 118 workers, the study found that servant leadership has a significant positive effect on service recovery performance and a significant negative effect on bullying. Key recommendations include modeling servant leadership behaviors through empathy, active listening, and giving staff decision-making authority.
papers.file: /research/Consequences of Servant Leadership on  Service Recovery Performance and Bullying.pdf

papers.title: Impact of Work Social Support on Tardiness and Turnover Intention
papers.abstract: This study investigates the impact and correlation of work social support on tardiness and turnover intention among employees in private hospitals in Sulaymaniyah City, Kurdistan Region of Iraq. A quantitative survey was conducted with a sample of 153 hospital employees. The study found a significant negative effect of work social support on turnover intention, recommending that organizations foster supportive team environments and supervisor relationships to reduce fatigue and the stress that leads to tardiness and employee turnover.
papers.file: /research/IMPACT OF WORK SOCIAL SUPPORT ON TARDINESS AND TURNOVER INTENTION.pdf

papers.title: Impact of Resonant Leadership on Employee Positive Affectivity in Private Hospitals in Sulaymaniyah City
papers.abstract: This study examines the effects of resonant leadership — defined by mindfulness, compassion, and emotional intelligence — on employees' positive affectivity in private hospitals in Sulaymaniyah, Kurdistan Region of Iraq. A quantitative research design was used with data collected via a standardized questionnaire issued to hospital personnel. The study contributes to the literature on leadership and emotional intelligence in the Kurdish healthcare context, providing practical ideas for improving employee morale and organizational commitment.
papers.file: /research/IMPACT OF RESONANT LEADERSHIP ON EMPLOYEE POSITIVE AFFECTIVITY IN PRIVATE HOSPITAL IN SULAYMANIYAH CITY KURDISTAN REGION-IRAQ.pdf

papers.title: Psychosocial and Ergonomic Factors Affecting Safety Performance in Local Construction Sites in Sulaimani City
papers.abstract: This study aims to understand the level of safety awareness among managers and identify factors influencing the implementation of safety policies in workplaces, focusing on 12 construction projects in Sulaimani City with a sample population of 235 managers. The study found that approximately 60% of respondents received no training on lifting heavy materials, and 51% engaged in frequent bending. Recommendations include implementing stress management workshops, problem-solving training, and fostering a positive organizational culture through open communication and employee recognition.
papers.file: /research/Psychosocial and Ergonomic Factors Affecting Safety Performance In The Local Construction Sites In Sulaimani City.pdf

papers.title: Microbial Contamination of Fresh Raw Vegetable Samples from Local Markets of Sulaymaniyah City
papers.abstract: This study evaluates microbial contamination in fresh raw vegetables obtained from local markets in Sulaymaniyah city. Vegetable samples were examined using microbiological and parasitological methods to identify possible contaminants. The results showed the presence of bacteria such as E. coli, Klebsiella, and Pseudomonas, indicating potential health risks for consumers. Antibiotic testing revealed differences in bacterial resistance. These findings emphasize the importance of proper hygiene, washing practices, and monitoring to ensure food safety and reduce the risk of foodborne diseases.
papers.file: /research/MICROBIAL CONTAMINATION OF FRESH RAW VEGETABLE SAMPLES COLLECTED FROM LOCAL MARKETS OF SULAYMANIYAH CITY.pdf

papers.title: Drilling Rig Model and Drilling Processes
papers.abstract: This study aims to precisely assess the environmental impact and characterize the key operations carried out during oil well drilling. Petroleum, a naturally occurring liquid mixture of hydrocarbons, is extracted primarily through oil drilling — a high-risk, financially invested process where profitability depends on efficiency and safety. The proper sizing and management of these processes are critical, and the primary tools utilized in a well's drilling process are discussed for a better understanding of all operations, concluding with a detailed drilling program for the well completion process.

papers.title: Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drugs and Organ Damage
papers.abstract: Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) are commonly utilized prescriptions to ease pain and inflammation. Despite their widespread use, they are known to produce adverse effects including gastrointestinal, cardiovascular, and renal toxicity. Studies have recommended a relationship between NSAID use and organ damage: gastrointestinal difficulties such as bleeding and ulcers are most commonly reported, while cardiovascular complications including myocardial infarction and stroke have also been documented. NSAIDs may inhibit cyclooxygenase enzymes, reducing prostaglandin production and prompting increased leukotriene creation that can cause tissue damage.

papers.title: The Impact of Cosmetics Advertising on the Cultural Identity and Consumption Patterns of Kurdish Consumers
papers.abstract: This study investigates the impact of cosmetics advertising on purchasing patterns and cultural identity amongst Kurdish college students in the Kurdistan-Iraqi region. A sample of 118 students from Komar University provided data through a survey. The results indicated robust correlations between cosmetics marketing impact, consumption styles, and cultural identity. Independent-samples t-tests found significant differences between male and female responses, with women reporting stronger advertising influences. Regression analyses showed that consumption patterns and cultural identification significantly stimulated the perceived effect of cosmetics advertising.
papers.file: /research/The Impact of Cosmetics Advertising on the Cultural Identity and Consumption Patterns of Kurdish Consumers.pdf

papers.title: Impact of Total Quality Management on Employee Career Adaptability: A Case Study in IceStar Ice Cream Factory
papers.abstract: This study explores the relationship between Total Quality Management (TQM) practices and employee career adaptability within the IceStar Ice Cream factory in Sulaymaniyah, Kurdistan Region of Iraq. A quantitative design was employed with data collected from a stratified random sample of 75 employees. Using SPSS, findings confirmed a significant positive correlation between TQM and career adaptability, with regression results demonstrating that TQM practices exert a significant positive effect on employees' career adaptability. The study recommends organizations adopt TQM as a strategic approach to enhance both organizational outcomes and employee development.
papers.file: /research/IMPACT_ OF_ TOTAL_ QUALITY_ MANAGEMENT_ ON_ EMPLOYEE'S_ CAREER_ ADAPTABILITY A_ CASE_ STUDY_ IN_ ICESTAR_ ICE_ CREAM_ FACTORY.pdf

papers.title: In Silico Design of Anticancer Peptides
papers.abstract: Anticancer peptides (ACPs) are a promising class of pharmaceuticals that are selective for cancer cells, have low toxicity, and are simple to synthesize. Cationic antimicrobial peptides (CAPs), a subtype of ACPs, have demonstrated strong anticancer action by causing cancer cell death and rupturing cell membranes. This review examines in silico methods for the design and optimization of ACPs, discussing drawbacks of computational approaches including the absence of experimental validation, and covers promising therapeutic targets such as p53, Bcl-2, and EGFR proteins, as well as PI3K/AKT/mTOR and MAPK pathways.

papers.title: Analysis of Regular and Premium Gasoline and Their Environmental Impacts in Sulaymaniyah Region
papers.abstract: This study observes gasoline types (premium and regular) in Sulaymaniyah city by taking samples from 7 stations in different locations, examined in a laboratory test station in Tanjero area. Three devices were used: Eravap for measuring vapor pressure, Eralytics multi-fuel analyzer, and Zetlex octane tester. A portable gas analyzer was used to determine greenhouse gases released during combustion. Results show that Darwaza gas station gasoline has the highest octane rating by utilizing ethanol and MTBE additives, resulting in less environmental impact, though gasoline combustion remains harmful through air pollution from toxic gas emissions.
papers.file: /research/Analysis of regular and premium gasoline and their environmental impacts in Sulaymaniyah region.pdf

papers.title: Solubility Improvement by Phase Solubility Analysis of Poorly Water-Soluble Drugs: Ibuprofen Complex Formation with Beta-Cyclodextrin
papers.abstract: This study investigates the utilization of Beta-cyclodextrin (BCD) as an excipient to improve the solubility of Ibuprofen, a poorly water-soluble medication. The findings uncovered that BCD significantly expanded the solubility of Ibuprofen, with a maximum solubility of 45.5 mg/mL achieved at a BCD to Ibuprofen weight ratio of 1:1, representing a four-fold expansion in solubility. The inclusion complex was characterized through PXRD, ITC, and UV-Vis spectroscopy, forming a stable 1:1 complex with positive enthalpy change, demonstrating BCD's capability as an excipient for improving solubility of poorly water-soluble drugs.
papers.file: /research/Solubility improvement by Phase solubility analyzing of poorly water-soluble drugs with the excipient-Ibuprofen complex formation with B-cyclodextrin.pdf
